Cluster (colour)No. of sequences (<70% id)OccurrenceSubfamily (CDD ID)Representative species (PDB structure)Polymerase functionConservation of motif DxE in the 3'–5' exonuclease domainAdditional domains or binding partners
1 (lime)4560■ Bacteria (all major phyla)Canonical Pol I (cd08637)■ E. coli (1D8Y)■ Lagging strand synthesisPartial (~42% sequences with catalytic residues conserved)■ 5'–3' exonuclease (94% sequences, N-terminal fusion)
■ G. stearothermophilus (1L3S)■ Single-strand gap repair
■ T. aquaticus (1TAQ)■ Removal of RNA primers
■ M. smegmatis (6VDD)■ Replication of plasmids
2 (silver)313■ Mitochondria (opisthokonts)Pol γ (cd08641)■ H. sapiens (31KM)■ Replication of mitochondrial DNAYes (all except some Glomeromycetes)■ Interacts with PolyB dimer (H. sapiens), monomer (D. melanogaster) or functions as a single subunit (S. cerevisiae)
■ Several cyanophages (Caudovirales)■ Phages A-HIS1, A-HIS2■ Replication of phage DNA (putative)
3 (lavender blue)306■ Phages (Caudovirales)SPO2-like (cd08642)■ Phage SPO2■ Replication of phage DNAYes (all)■ No known partners
■ Prophages mainly in Firmicutes
4 (light red)290■ Bacteria (Actinobacteria); does not replace canonical Pol IAPEX (established in this study)■ S. coelicolor Streptomyces sp. CT34■ UV sensitivity reductionNo; exo catalytic pocket is tightly sealed■ No known partners
■ Confirmed polymerase activity (this study)
■ DNA damage repair (probable)
5 (cyan)205■ Phages (Caudovirales)T7-like (cd08643)■ Phage T7 (2AJQ; in a replisome complex: 51KN)■ Replication of phage DNAYes (all)■ Interacts with host's thioredoxin through TBD insertion on thumb's tip (~29% sequences with TBD ≥50 amino acids)
■ Prophages mainly in Proteobacteria■ Phage S-SBP1■ Interacts with TOPRIM primase-helicase (shown for T7)
■ Separate 3'–5' exonuclease domain (S-SBP1-like)
6 (yellow)143■ EukaryotesPol θ (cd08638)■ H. sapiens (4 × 0Q)■ DNA repair (MMEJ)No■ Superfamily 2 helicase (HELQ, N-terminal fusion)
■ Template-dependent and -independent synthesis
7 (light blue)76■ Mitochondria and plastids (non-opisthokonts); replaced by Pol γ in opisthokontsPOPs (cd08640)■ A. thaliana■ Replication and repair of organellar DNAYes (~89% sequences)■ 5'–3' exonuclease (~8% sequences, N-terminal fusion)
8 (dark green)75■ Diverse bacteria (mainly Aquificae and Cyanobacteria); replaces canonical Pol I in some AquificaeHot Spring 1: Aquificae-like (cd08639)■ A. aeolicus■ Unknown role in prokaryotesYes (~93% sequences)■ AEP primase-polymerase (~7% of bacterial sequences, N-terminal fusion)
■ M. vulcani■ Replication of apicoplast DNA
■ P. falciparum (5DKU)■ Thermostable■ Polyprotein in Apicomplexa: fused to TOPRIM primase and helicase (N-terminal)
9 (purple red)74■ Actinomycetia phages (Caudovirales)D29-like (established in this study)■ Mycobacterium phage D29■ Replication of phage DNA (putative)Yes (~99% sequences)■ No known partners
■ Actinomycetia prophages
10 (pale pink)68■ Diverse bacteria (mainly Acidobacteria and candidate division WWE3); does not replace canonical Pol IHot Spring 2 (shares a recent common ancestor with Aquificae-like)■ Pyrinomonas methylaliphatogenes■ Unknown roleYes (~96% sequences)■ No known partners
■ Diverse archaea (metagenomics-derived, putative)■ Thermostable (putative)
11 (pink-red)59■ Phages (Caudovirales)φJL001-like■ Phage φJL001■ Replication of phage DNA (putative)Yes (~98% sequences)■ No known partners
12 (lilac)59■ Phages (Caudovirales)KPP25-like (established in this study)■ Phage KPP25■ UntestedYes (all)■ No known partners
■ Prophages mainly in Proteobacteria■ Replication of phage DNA (putative)
13 (aquamarine)59■ Phages of Proteobacteria (Caudovirales)phiKMV-like (established in this study)■ Phage phiKMV■ Replication of phage DNA (putative)Yes (~95% sequences)■ ~100 amino acid insertion on thumb's tip, unrelated to TBD
■ Proteobacteria prophages

15 (dark violet)52■ Bacteria (mainly Proteobacteria); does not replace canonical Pol I ■ Euryarchaeota (Methanomicrobia) ■ Related to CCPols from staphylococcal MGEsCHEAPs (established in this study)■ I. dechloratans■ Confirmed polymerase activity (this study) ■ Highly efficient polymerase and exonuclease activities ■ Replication-related (probable)Yes (~88% sequences)■ No known partners ■ CCPols lack the majority of 3′–5′ exonuclease domain and form a primase-helicase complex with MD, Cch2
